displayTitle: Three Ways to Wrap a Coil Around a Part
kicker: ET LEVEL I // PROBE COIL ARRANGEMENTS

beats.narration: The physics never changes: a coil carries alternating current. What changes job to job is how it's arranged around the part.

payload.title: PROBE COIL ARRANGEMENTS
payload.sub: Same coil, three ways to meet the part.

beats.narration: Geometry makes the choice. A surface probe presses flat against the part, an encircling coil rings it, an inside bobbin travels the bore.
Each arrangement steers the currents differently. Surface coils drive circular loops; encircling and inside coils drive currents around the circumference of the part.

tiles.s: Currents ring the wall, from the bore

beats.narration: Coverage differs too. A surface probe sees only its footprint, so you overlap passes. Encircling and bobbin coils read a full circle at once.

chips.s: Bar, rod, wire, tubing feed through

beats.narration: One rule governs every choice: a flaw signals only when it cuts across the induced current paths. Ask which way the currents flow first.

lines.t: CROSS THEM TO SEE THE FLAW.

payload.sub: A flaw shows only when it cuts the current paths.
